title: Official welcome ceremony held for Czech Prime Minister
body: On April 30, an official welcome ceremony was held at the Kuksaroy Residence for the Prime Minister of the Czech Republic Andrej Babiš, who is on an official visit to Uzbekistan. A guard of honor was lined up in the square outside the residence. President Shavkat Mirziyoyev warmly welcomed the distinguished guest and invited him to the podium. The military band performed the national anthems of both countries. The leaders inspected the guard of honor and greeted members of the official delegations. Following the ceremony and a joint photo session, high-level negotiations began. Photo: Press Service of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan\

title: Uzbek–Czech relations enter a new stage of development
body: Negotiations were held between the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev, and the Prime Minister of the Czech Republic Andrej Babiš, in a narrow format and with the participation of the two countries’ delegations at the Kuksaroy Residence. The sides discussed the current state and prospects for further development of practical cooperation between Uzbekistan and the Czech Republic. At the beginning of the meeting, the President emphasized that the current visit would be a breakthrough and would open a qualitatively new stage in the history of multifaceted Uzbek–Czech relations. The dynamic development of cooperation across all areas was noted with satisfaction. Close contacts have been established at the level of governments, ministries, and agencies, while business and humanitarian exchanges have intensified. Friendship groups have been established in the parliaments, and the Intergovernmental Commission is operating effectively. Bilateral trade turnover has nearly doubled in recent years. There are currently 37 joint ventures with Czech capital successfully operating in Uzbekistan, and cooperation is expanding across a broad range of new areas. Particular attention was paid to increasing mutual trade. A target was set to raise trade turnover to $1 billion, including by expanding the range of traded goods. To stimulate trade, the first Uzbek certification branch is being established in the Czech Republic. In cooperation with Czech partners, a modern laboratory for the certification of Euro-6 standard vehicles and quantum measurement standards is also under construction. An agreement was reached to prepare a Technological Cooperation Program with leading Czech companies, including the implementation of projects in mechanical engineering, green energy, geology and critical raw materials, chemistry, pharmaceuticals, and other sectors. Prospects for cooperation in infrastructure development, the creation of smart cities, engineering, and digitalization were also noted. In this context, the project initiatives presented at the Uzbek–Czech Business Forum held the previous day were supported. The sides welcomed the intentions of the Export Credit Insurance Agency and the Export Bank of the Czech Republic to support project implementation in Uzbekistan. To advance the economic agenda, it was proposed to establish a Business Council and to hold the next meeting of the Intergovernmental Commission in Tashkent this August. In the cultural and humanitarian sphere, both sides confirmed their interest in expanding educational and academic exchanges, including through dual degree programs. The importance of regularly holding cultural and film days, exhibitions, and concerts, as well as increasing tourist flows, was emphasized. To this end, the resumption of direct flights between the capitals of the two countries will be explored. The prospects for cooperation in the field of organized labor migration were also discussed separately. The sides also exchanged views on issues on the international agenda. In conclusion, an agreement was reached to prepare a joint roadmap for the timely implementation of the decisions adopted. Photo: Press Service of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan

title: Uzbekistan and the Czech Republic sign package of bilateral agreements
body: Following talks held in Tashkent, President of the Republic of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev and Prime Minister of the Czech Republic Andrej Babiš signed a Joint Declaration on the promotion of expanded cooperation. A signing ceremony for bilateral agreements was also held, including cooperation in economic, industrial, scientific, and technological fields, the training of diplomatic personnel, joint design and support for the establishment of a metrology laboratory, scientific and innovative cooperation in metrology, cooperation in geology, as well as the supply of electric trains. Photo: Press Service of the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan

title: Meeting with Students of Turin Polytechnic University: Industry Insights and the Future of Aviation
body: The development of national aviation is impossible without the influx of young, ambitious talent. Yesterday, on April 30, the Chairman of the Board of Uzbekistan Airways JSC, Shukhrat Khudaykulov, held an open and inspiring meeting with students from Turin Polytechnic University in Tashkent. The event was held in a format of sincere and lively dialogue. Students from various faculties demonstrated a deep understanding of global aviation trends, boldly exchanging views and asking the leadership the most pressing questions. Future specialists were interested in a serious range of topics. During the discussion, they explored opportunities for professional internships within the airline’s structures, the strategic criteria and processes for opening new scheduled routes. Additionally, participants touched upon the intricacies of acquiring modern aircraft, the specifics of lending and leasing agreements, and the fundamental principles of ensuring flight safety. Addressing the youth, Shukhrat Khudaykulov emphasized that the airline's success is built on the coordinated work of a massive mechanism, where every employee is vital – from pilots and flight attendants to strategists, commercial department specialists, and the Flight Operations Center. emphasized Shukhrat Khudaykulov, noting that success is built on the coordinated work of the entire team. At the conclusion of the meeting, the most active participants in the discussion received branded gifts from the national carrier. The leadership sincerely thanked the students for their passion and engagement, emphasizing that such open conversations lay a solid foundation for the development of a new, strong generation of aviators in our country.

title: Uzbekistan Airways Successfully Completes 10th IOSA Audit (IATA Operational Safety Audit)
body: Uzbekistan Airways JSC has successfully passed its 10th IOSA (IATA Operational Safety Audit). The airline first received the IOSA certificate in 2008 and has since confirmed its compliance with international operational safety standards every two years. The results of the latest audit confirm the airline's steady compliance with international standards and recommended practices in flight safety, as well as the effectiveness of its implemented safety management system. The consistent successful completion of IOSA audits reflects the systematic approach of Uzbekistan Airways JSC toward maintaining and continuously improving flight safety, operational reliability, and service quality. The airline will continue to implement its flight safety and quality policy in accordance with international requirements and global industry best practices.

title: New Horizons of Comfort: Pay for Uzbekistan Airways Tickets and Services via WeChat
body: The national airline, Uzbekistan Airways, is continuously working to make your travels even more convenient and accessible. We strive to implement advanced digital solutions that save your time and provide confidence at every stage of trip planning, wherever you may be. We are pleased to announce that a new option is now available to our passengers — fast and secure payment for air tickets and additional airline services using the popular international platform, WeChat. All transactions within this system are processed directly in CNY (Yuan), making the purchasing process as transparent and comfortable as possible for the service users. We understand the importance of having familiar and reliable financial tools at your fingertips in today’s dynamic world. Integration with the WeChat payment system is another significant step by the airline toward innovation, making the booking of your next trip a matter of just a few minutes. Experience new standards of convenience, plan your routes with ease, and discover the world with us. Comfortable journeys begin with Uzbekistan Airways!

title: Uzbekistan Airways is moving to a new terminal at Frankfurt Airport
body: Starting May 7, the airline’s flights will operate to and from Terminal 3, which opened after 11 years of construction. The new terminal is designed to handle up to 19 million passengers a year and offers a high level of comfort for travelers. We ask passengers to take this change into account when planning their trip and to check the departure and arrival terminal information in advance.

title: Honoring the Heroes: Uzbekistan Airways Paid Tribute to the Defenders of the Peaceful Sky
body: On the eve of the Day of Remembrance and Honor, employees and veterans of Uzbekistan Airways paid tribute to the heroes who selflessly fought for our bright future, peace, and well-being. Memorial events were held simultaneously at several landmark locations in Tashkent: Victory Park, the “Brotherly Graves” memorial complex, as well as the city’s “Chigatai” and “Minor” cemeteries. Participants laid flowers and paid deep respect to the fallen soldiers. On this day, memories of the hardships of the war years were shared, recalling how the incredible unity, courage, and faith of our people laid a solid foundation for the peaceful development of independent Uzbekistan. The gathering held special significance for the continuity of generations. The airline's young specialists had the opportunity to see historical models of aviation equipment that saved hundreds of lives by performing the most complex evacuation and defense missions. Listening to the eyewitness stories of the older generation, the youth were once again reminded of the immense price of the words most sacred to every aviator and citizen: a peaceful sky and a peaceful country. We remember. We are proud.

title: A Historic Event in the Civil Aviation of the Republic of Uzbekistan: A New Stage in the Development of Uzbekistan Airways Technics
body: Today, May 8, a truly historic event took place at our structural division, Uzbekistan Airways Technics. The enterprise’s highly qualified specialists successfully performed complex On-Wing engine testing following the completion of maintenance works. This process was conducted under the strict supervision of an official representative from the General Electric (GE) Corporation. The significance of this event is further highlighted by the fact that the tests were carried out directly on a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. This major achievement was preceded by extensive collaborative work with General Electric. The airline’s specialists completed an intensive training course, obtained relevant international certifications, and demonstrated flawless mastery of Engine Split procedures, as well as the mating and unmating of GEnx-1B engine components. By implementing this vast complex of preparatory measures, Uzbekistan Airways Technics has confidently moved to the next, higher stage of its technical evolution. – “The project, which launched in 2023, has become a vital step in the development of the strategic partnership between Uzbekistan Airways and General Electric. To successfully master this cutting-edge technology, we sent our Uzbekistan Airways Technics specialists for intensive training in Doha. Over three months, they not only enhanced their qualifications but also earned prestigious international certifications. I sincerely thank every employee of our aviation enterprise and our colleagues from General Electric for this breakthrough and their dedication to our shared cause,” noted Farkhod Ibragimov, Director of Uzbekistan Airways Technics LLC. – “The implementation of tasks at this level requires a highly professional team with extensive practical experience. Together with the specialists from Uzbekistan Airways Technics, we have accomplished truly monumental work. This successful project not only strengthens our partnership but also allows us to significantly optimize aircraft maintenance turnaround times,” emphasized Igor Zabelin, Chief Engineer of the Civil Aviation Maintenance Department at GE Aviation. The successful execution of this innovative program elevates the prestige and image of the enterprise – which boasts a century-long history – to a completely new international level. Today, Uzbekistan Airways Technics proudly offers a wide range of aircraft maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services for the entire Central Asian region. It is worth noting that Uzbekistan Airways Technics is part of the Uzbekistan Airways Group. Our airline proudly holds the status of the only carrier in the region operating the Boeing 787 Dreamliner. Furthermore, Uzbekistan Airways remains the only airline across the CIS to operate regular, direct transatlantic flights to New York. – “Previously, the full cycle of shipping, repairing abroad, and returning this engine took anywhere from three to six months. The transportation itself was a complex logistical challenge, requiring specialized heavy-lift cargo aircraft. Now, thanks to the mastered technology, the engine can be transported in a disassembled state on standard cargo aircraft types. This radically simplifies logistics and enables us to resolve complex technical and operational tasks as promptly as possible,” concluded Minur Beshatov, Director of the Continuing Airworthiness Department at Uzbekistan Airways. Behind every safe and comfortable flight lies the immense effort of a vast team: the century-long experience of the region's largest enterprise, Uzbekistan Airways Technics, and the cutting-edge innovations of our Training Center, which continuously educates a new generation of professional aviators in strict accordance with the highest European EASA standards.

title: The term of office of Shuhrat Shavkatovich Khudaykulov as Chairman of the Board has been extended for another three year
body: By decision of the Supervisory Board and the General Meeting of Shareholders of Uzbekistan Airways JSC, the term of office of Shukhrat Shavkatovich Khudaykulov as Chairman of the Management Board has been extended for another three years. Shukhrat Shavkatovich has been heading the national airline since 2023. Taking into account the achieved results and the ongoing development strategy, the shareholders expressed their confidence in the current management by supporting the continuation of his tenure. The decision is aimed at the further development of the airline, expansion of the route network, and improvement of service quality. Under his leadership, projects on fleet modernization, strengthening international cooperation, and increasing operational efficiency are being consistently implemented.

title: Uzbekistan Airways is taking part “OTDYKH”
body: Uzbekistan Airways is taking part in the International Tourism Fair “OTDYKH”, held in Minsk from April 9 to 11. Participation in one of the region’s key industry events has become an important platform for strengthening professional ties and promoting the tourism potential of the airline’s destinations. At the Uzbekistan Airways branded stand, visitors can explore the route network, learn more about current offers and services, and receive personalized консультации from the airline’s representatives. Particular attention is given to the business program: the exhibition features extensive and productive B2B meetings with partners and representatives of the tourism industry. Discussions focus on prospects for cooperation, route network development, and new opportunities for mutual growth. Participation in the “OTDYKH” exhibition once again confirms Uzbekistan Airways’ commitment to expanding its international presence, strengthening partnerships, and creating even more comfortable travel conditions for passengers. We also invite you to visit our stand at the BelExpo Exhibition Center (Hall 5)!

title: Meeting with the youth
body: Managers of the tourism and marketing departments of Uzbekistan Airways held a meeting with students of the Turin Polytechnic University in Tashkent. The meeting was attended by Business Management students, who showed strong interest in the airline’s activities. During the discussion, participants asked a wide range of questions, and representatives of Uzbekistan Airways provided detailed and comprehensive answers. In particular, the discussion covered topics related to the airline’s marketing strategy, service promotion mechanisms, and passenger flow management. Opportunities for internships in areas such as crisis management, emergency response, marketing, and business were also discussed. Students were also interested in the key skills required for a career in marketing, as well as the expectations placed on young professionals. Special attention was given to aviation-related topics, including spare parts supply and the differences between Airbus and Boeing aircraft. In addition, participants asked about aircraft capacity and the specifics of various professions in the aviation industry. The most active students were recognized with commemorative gifts. The meeting was held in an open and constructive atmosphere, further strengthening dialogue with future industry professionals.

title: Presentation at the Uzbekistan-Malaysia Forum
body: Uzbekistan Airways gave a presentation at the Uzbekistan-Malaysia forum “Connecting Regions” in Malacca, organised in collaboration with the Embassy of the Republic of Uzbekistan in Malaysia. The event brought together representatives of travel companies and the business community from Uzbekistan and Malaysia, and served as a platform to showcase the country’s tourism potential, with a focus on new travel packages to Samarkand, Bukhara and other cities.

title: Khan-Atlas in the Rhythm of Business Life
body: Employees of the head office of Uzbekistan Airways and the Uzbekistan Airways Sales branch seamlessly combine business style with elements of national culture, highlighting respect for traditions and national identity. Khan-atlas holds a special place in their attire – a vibrant and distinctive fabric that adds a striking national accent to classic office wear. Such details not only enhance appearance but also reflect the rich cultural heritage of Uzbekistan, making the corporate style truly unique.

title: Your best memories shouldn't have to wait!
body: They say that travel is the only thing you buy that makes you richer. But how often do we put our dreams on hold? After seeing a ticket price, we close the tab with a sigh, thinking: "now isn't the time for extra expenses" — and we go back to waiting for those "better times." Does that sound familiar? With Uzbekistan Airways, your dreams come true today! You no longer need to postpone seeing your loved ones until next year or save up for months for a family vacation. We have shortened the distance between you and your goal to just a few clicks: With our new installment service, we take the financial weight off your shoulders by dividing the ticket cost into comfortable, almost unnoticeable payments. You book your ticket today, and tomorrow you're sipping coffee overlooking the Bosphorus or strolling down the Champs-Élysées. The best part is left to you — choosing your destination and enjoying the flight. Applying for installments is done entirely online and takes less time than finishing your morning tea.

title: Take more luggage to New York – at no extra charge!
body: From February 1 to April 30, 2026, an increased baggage allowance will be in effect on direct flights from Tashkent to New York to Tashkent in economy class. ➖for refundable fares – 2 pieces up to 30 kg/158 cm each ➖for non-refundable fares – 1 piece up to 30 kg/158 cm This offer is valid regardless of the date of purchase of the ticket. Choose Uzbekistan Airways for affordable flights! Flights from Tashkent to New York are available on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Sundays, and from April, flights on Fridays and Saturdays will be added.

title: +23 kg of baggage as a gift on flights to Seoul!
body: Until March 28, 2026, passengers on Tashkent – Seoul – Tashkent flights will receive a bonus – free transportation of +1 piece of baggage up to 23 kg in addition to the established norm. The offer is valid for refundable and non-refundable economy class fares, as well as business class fares. We fly to Seoul daily on comfortable Boeing 787 aircraft! Tickets are available at booking.uzairways.com and in the app.

title: Uzbekistan Airways participates in TITF-2026
body: Uzbekistan Airways has taken part in one of the region's largest tourism exhibitions – the Thailand International Travel Fair (TITF-2026). Currently held in Bangkok, the fair brings together key representatives from the global tourism and aviation industries. During the event, Uzbekistan Airways presented its current and future offerings for the Thai market, highlighting the potential for expanding tourism, business, and cultural exchanges between Uzbekistan and Thailand. On the very first day, the event’s attendance, including visitors to our stand, exceeded 30,000 people. This high turnout demonstrates a strong interest in tourism destinations and air connectivity between the two nations. Participation in TITF-2026 is a key strategic event for the Southeast Asian region, aimed at strengthening partnerships and promoting Uzbekistan as an attractive tourist destination. This initiative also facilitates an increase in tourist flow, aligning with the objectives set by the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Uzbekistan Airways continues to actively develop its presence in the Thailand market by expanding cooperation with local partners and offering passengers new opportunities for comfortable travel.

title: Uzbekistan Airways is participating in one of the largest international tourism exhibitions, FITUR, which is being held in Madrid.
body: As part of the exhibition, airline representatives are informing visitors about current fares, route network development, and aviation opportunities. A series of business meetings with representatives of local businesses, travel companies, and partners are also planned, aimed at strengthening cooperation. Participation in FITUR allows Uzbekistan Airways to promote the country's tourism potential on the international stage, which is in line with the objectives set by the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Regular flights to Madrid will be operated on a weekly basis, every Monday, starting from the spring-summer navigation season.

title: The “Lucky Ticket” promotion by Uzbekistan Airways!
body: Taking part is easy: from January 26 to 29, purchase tickets for any Uzbekistan Airways flights on booking.uzairways.com or via the mobile app. Winners will receive a free round-trip flight for two to destinations such as Kuala Lumpur, Istanbul, Tbilisi or Batumi, Moscow, or any other regular destination served by the airline. To find out the results, join our live broadcasts on January 30, 2026, on Instagram or Telegram.

title: PUBLIC OFFER on Conducting the "Lucky Ticket" Promotional Campaign
body: This document constitutes an official proposal (Public Offer) by "Uzbekistan Airways" JSC (hereinafter referred to as the "Organizer") to an unlimited number of persons to participate in the "Lucky Ticket" promotional campaign (hereinafter referred to as the "Campaign") under the terms and conditions set forth below. Participation in the Campaign signifies the participant's full and unconditional acceptance of all terms of this Offer.

title: Uzbekistan Airways successfully presented its capabilities at the Travel Adventure Show in New York!
body: Participation in the international exhibition allowed the airline to strengthen its brand recognition in the North American market, establish new business contacts with representatives of the tourism industry, and expand prospects for cooperation with international partners. Regular flights from Tashkent to New York are operated on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Sundays, and from April, flights on Fridays and Saturdays will be added.

title: Uzbekistan Airways participates in a key global event in the field of aviation finance and leasing
body: The Airline Economics Growth Frontiers conference is taking place in Dublin, bringing together industry leaders, airline representatives, lessors, and investors. Participants are discussing current market trends and sharing cutting-edge financial solutions. The focus is on aircraft financing and leasing, sale-and-leaseback schemes, fleet management, ESG, and strategic trends in the aviation market. Dublin is traditionally considered a global center for aviation leasing and financial expertise. The event format includes panel discussions, practical case studies, and active networking with key industry players. Representatives of Uzbekistan Airways actively participate in business sessions and meetings, strengthening international cooperation and exploring new opportunities for the development of the company's financial strategies. Participation in the conference allows Uzbekistan Airways not only to exchange experience with leading global experts, but also to demonstrate its achievements in fleet management and financial planning. The airline is strengthening its position in the international arena, expanding its network of partnerships, and forming a strategic vision for the further development of the aviation business.

title: It’s our birthday and you get the gifts
body: We inform you about promotion campaign on January, 28 2026 in honor of celebration of the 34th anniversary of Uzbekistan Airways on all HY operating flights (include domestic flights) according to which a 30% discount of individual business and economy fares is applied: Required Ticket Designator please check below. When reissuing/rebooking air tickets on international routes, it is permitted to use a discount of 15% of the fare (in accordance with information message No. 6.6.3432 dated January 12, 2026) only if reissue is made in the period from January 29, 2026 to March 13, 2026 for flights from January 29, 2026 to March 28, 2026;

title: Uzbekistan Airways at the Roadshow in Kuala Lumpur
body: The B2B Roadshow “Uzbekistan and Central Asia” was held in Kuala Lumpur, organized by the representative office of Uzbekistan Airways in Malaysia in conjunction with Smart Global Adventures. The event was attended by about 10 tour operators from Uzbekistan and more than 100 representatives of the Malaysian tourism industry. During the meeting, Uzbekistan Airways talked about its aviation capabilities, route network, and flight frequency. Today, January 29, the B2B Roadshow series will continue in Singapore. Flights on the Tashkent – Kuala Lumpur – Tashkent route are operated by Boeing 787 Dreamliners on Wednesdays and Sundays, with return flights on Mondays and Thursdays.

title: Uzbekistan Airways is participating in the IMTM-2026 international Mediterranean tourism fair in Tel Aviv
body: On the first day of the exhibition, the airline's stand attracted increased attention from numerous visitors, including both tourism professionals and potential travelers. Representatives of Uzbekistan Airways are holding a series of business meetings and negotiations with partners. Participation in IMTM-2026 allows the airline to promote the tourism potential of Uzbekistan on the international arena, which is in line with the objectives set by the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan. As a reminder, flights on the Tashkent – Tel Aviv – Tashkent route are operated regularly on Mondays and Thursdays.

title: Uzbekistan Airways Showcases Potential at OTM 2026 in Mumbai
body: From February 5 to 7, 2026, the OTM 2026 (Outbound Travel Mart), a premier international travel exhibition, took place at the Jio World Convention Centre in Mumbai, India. As Asia’s leading B2B travel platform, OTM brings together over 2,000 exhibitors from 60+ countries, focusing on both outbound and domestic tourism. Throughout the event, the airline’s stand attracted significant interest from over 1,000 participants and guests, ranging from small-scale agencies to major industry players. Among them were representatives from prominent Indian tour operators, including Veena Travels, Thomas Cook, and Akbar Travels. The exhibition provided a valuable opportunity to establish business contacts with renowned Indian entrepreneurs. The exhibition featured a wide array of sectors, including: • Tourism products and luxury travel; • MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions); • Beach and family tourism. OTM 2026 stands as a key event for fostering partnerships and strengthening ties within the travel industry. It offered travel agents a platform to build business relationships with local operators and present their products to the Indian market. We are confident that participation in this exhibition will facilitate the airline's entry into new markets and help build strategic business connections with international partners.

title: Address by the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev at the First Summit of the Board of Peace
body: Allow me to express my to the President of the United States of America, His Excellency Donald Trump for convening inaugural meeting of the Board. Indeed, today's summit is the result of President Trump’s political will and pragmatism. Uzbekistan has supported the peacebuilding initiative of establishing the Board of Peace, and firmly declared its commitment to take practical part in its successful implementation. I am confident that the Board’s work will create a favorable condition for the economic and social restoration of Gaza. At the same time, any mechanism for external governance of Gaza must rely on the support of its population. Coordinated efforts of all interested parties will make the post-conflict process sustainable and create conditions for the rapid reconstruction of Gaza. I would like to emphasize that Uzbekistan is ready to make a tangible contribution to the construction of residential buildings, kindergartens, schools and hospitals. It is symbolic that our meeting is taking place at the United States Institute of Peace. Our meeting is a vivid manifestation of international solidarity, peacebuilding and a common aspiration to improve the situation in the Middle East. We need to use this historic opportunity to create a New Gaza with a thriving economy and decent living conditions for its population.

title: Uzbekistan Airways moves to Beijing Daxing Airport
body: Starting March 30, with the beginning of the spring-summer season, Uzbekistan Airways will operate flights to/from Beijing Daxing International Airport (PKX) instead of Beijing Capital International Airport (PEK). We kindly ask passengers to take this change into account when planning their trip and to check the departure and arrival terminal information in advance. Flights from Tashkent to Beijing will operate on Mondays and Thursdays, with an increase in flight frequency to follow. The current schedule and tickets are available on the website booking.uzairways.com and in the app.

title: The Uzbekistan Airways team performs well in the Team Championships
body: The 4th KLC Cup (CS2) has concluded! One of the tournament's biggest highlights was the Uzbekistan Airways team, proving that being a "favorite" doesn't matter when you have character, discipline, and team chemistry. Heading into the tournament, Uzbekistan Airways weren’t considered top contenders for the podium. However, our colleagues managed to impose their playstyle on nearly every opponent. The team demonstrated: • Precise aim and effective trade-fragging; • Clutch composure in high-pressure situations; • Well-prepared strategies on key maps; • The ability to adapt mid-game during BO3 series. In a fierce competition filled with comebacks and neck-and-neck rounds, the squad secured 4th place in the Gold Cup – a result that stands as a significant achievement. Crucially, the team didn't just "hold on" – they set the pace. Even against more experienced rivals, Uzbekistan Airways remained highly competitive, showcasing solid macro-play and disciplined site retakes. This tournament marks a major step forward for the roster: • Gaining valuable experience against top-tier teams; • Strengthening their mental fortitude; • Proving they can fight for the top spots. With this mindset and growth dynamic, Uzbekistan Airways has every chance to not only repeat this success but aim for the Grand Final in the next KLC Cup. The team has already shown they can compete on equal footing; now it’s all about consistency and fine-tuning their strategies. Fourth place is not the ceiling – it’s the launchpad. If this progress continues, Uzbekistan Airways is well-positioned to break into the Top 3 in future tournaments. GG WP! More thrilling matches and big victories lie ahead.

title: Hangzhou: Travel Together, Pay Less
body: Uzbekistan Airways is launching the special A4PAX fare for groups of 4 to 9 people on the Tashkent – Hangzhou – Tashkent route! The special A4PAXOW/-RT fare is available for issuance on flights from/to Tashkent to/from Hangzhou under the following conditions: This fare does not apply to BSP, ARC, or TKP agents. Booking violations: If there are fewer than 4 passengers in a booking, the agent will receive a formal penalty (ADM) for violating booking rules. Additionally, the agent will be charged the difference between the A4PAX fare and the non-refundable L-class fare, as well as the difference in the YQ (fuel) surcharge.

title: Uzbekistan Airways Fare Policy Update
body: To expand passenger choice and enhance the flexibility of air travel conditions, the airline is introducing branded fares that offer multiple ticket pricing tiers. Branded fares will differ in travel conditions and the set of included services (baggage allowance, ticket exchange and refund rules, the ability to change departure dates, and other service options), allowing passengers to choose the most suitable flight option. Effective April 1, 2026, branded fares are being introduced on domestic routes within the Republic of Uzbekistan, with minimum fare levels under the "Lite" brand: Fares are calculated based on the exchange rate as of March 17, 2026. The specified fares represent the minimum airfare levels and are subject to change depending on the date of ticket purchase, seat availability, and the applicable fare rules. A branded fare system is also being introduced on international routes, providing several airfare tiers in Economy and Business classes, which differ based on the set of included services and fare application rules. In the event of unutilized travel on non-refundable tickets, passengers will be refunded all taxes and fees for the unused sectors, excluding the fuel surcharge and the booking fee.

title: A double celebration of spring and warmth: fly to your loved ones with Uzbekistan Airways!
body: The brightest and most anticipated days are here — the holiday of spring renewal, Navruz, and the sacred Ramazan Hayit. This is a time when it is especially important to be close to family, visit elders, bring joy to loved ones, and share warmth with one another. To ensure you can easily and comfortably travel to your family and friends, Uzbekistan Airways is introducing additional domestic flights from March 19 to 23. Tickets for the new flights are already on sale! Furthermore, we would like to remind you of our festive promotion: until March 23, a discount of up to 15% off fares applies to all domestic flights. You can purchase tickets on the booking.uzairways.com website, at the Uzbekistan Airways Sales branch, and from the airline's official agents. Comfortable journeys begin with Uzbekistan Airways!

title: A Celebration of Spring and Tradition: Pre-Holiday Fair at Uzbekistan Airways Head Office
body: On the eve of the brightest and most anticipated spring holidays – Navruz and the sacred Ramazan Hayit – a truly warm and festive atmosphere has taken hold at our airline's head office. Today, a special festive food fair was organized for Uzbekistan Airways employees. This wonderful corporate initiative allows our large team to prepare for the upcoming long weekend in a comfortable and convenient setting. Colleagues were able to purchase fresh products and traditional treats for their festive dastarkhan, saving valuable time during the pre-holiday rush. A special flair and unique mood were brought to the day by the aviators themselves. Embracing the spirit of spring renewal and deep respect for our people's rich heritage, many employees arrived today wearing outfits with elements of national identity. The vibrant patterns of traditional fabrics and authentic accessories filled the office with an atmosphere of a true Uzbek holiday, comfort, and unity. Events like these do more than just help with holiday preparations; they incredibly strengthen team spirit, reminding us of the importance of preserving our shared cultural values. We sincerely congratulate everyone on the upcoming holidays! May these spring days bring peace, prosperity, joy, and abundance to every home.

title: Iftar is being organized in Malaysia
body: Representatives of Uzbekistan Airways in Kuala Lumpur organized an iftar for tour operators and gave a presentation aimed at promoting the airline’s services. During the event, participants were briefed on the airline’s route network and current offers, and they also discussed prospects for cooperation and the development of tourist traffic.

title: Uzbekistan Airways Technics – A Key Participant in the MRO Central Asia International Conference
body: From March 25 to 26, one of the most significant events in the field of aviation engineering is taking place: the ato/comm MRO 2026 Central Asia Uzbekistan international exhibition and conference. Specialists from Uzbekistan Airways and its subsidiary, Uzbekistan Airways Technics (UAT), are actively participating in the event. This event is entirely dedicated to the maintenance and repair (M&R) of aviation equipment. Today, it is the largest professional platform for sharing best practices, bringing together leading experts from Central Asia, Eurasia, the Caspian region, and the Caucasus. For Uzbekistan Airways Technics, participating in MRO 2026 is an excellent opportunity to clearly demonstrate the highest level of expertise and the strong potential of its technical center. As part of the business program, our specialists discuss the implementation of modern innovative solutions in the field of MRO, share unique expertise, and strengthen direct international cooperation with industry partners. Flawless maintenance is the foundation of safety and reliability. We are constantly improving our capabilities to ensure that every one of your flights is of the highest standard!

title: Dear passengers traveling to the People’s Republic of China
body: Please be reminded that prior to your flight you are required to complete the China Electronic Arrival Card – a mandatory requirement for all foreign nationals. You can fill it out in advance 24-72 hours before departure via the following link: https://s.nia.gov.cn/ArrivalCardFillingPhone/. After submission, you will receive a QR code. Please save it and present it upon arrival at the airport in China. If you are planning to travel to Beijing, Sanya, Urumqi, Hangzhou, or Shenzhen on Uzbekistan Airways flights, we strongly recommend taking this requirement into account.

title: Dear passengers traveling to India,
body: Starting from April 1, 2026, the use of paper immigration arrival cards (DE cards) in India will be discontinued. All foreign nationals are now required to complete an electronic arrival card (e-Arrival Card) in advance – within 72 hours prior to arrival. The form can be completed online via the Indian visa service website (indianvisaonline.gov.in/earrival) or through the Su-Swagatam mobile app. Please note that paper forms will remain valid only until March 31, 2026. If you are planning to travel to Delhi or Mumbai on Uzbekistan Airways flights, we kindly recommend completing the e-Arrival Card in advance to avoid delays during passport control.

title: More Travel Opportunities: Uzbekistan Airways and Air India Sign Codeshare Agreement
body: Uzbekistan Airways and Air India have signed a codeshare agreement aimed to expanding opportunities for passengers of both airlines and fostering international cooperation. Flights on the Tashkent – Delhi, Tashkent – Mumbai, and Tashkent – Goa (seasonal route) routes are available for booking under the codeshare agreement effective March 20, 2026. The partnership is designed to expand the capabilities of both airlines' route networks and strengthen air connectivity between Uzbekistan and India. The agreement also unlocks additional travel opportunities through key aviation hubs in India, providing passengers with convenient access to Uzbekistan Airways flights to destinations within Uzbekistan. Thanks to the codeshare agreement, passengers will be able to plan trips along these routes utilizing a single ticket, ensuring a more seamless travel experience and broader access to the route networks of both airlines. Tickets are already available on the website and at the sales offices of our partner, Air India.

title: New York is Getting Even Closer: Increasing Flight Frequency!
body: Dreaming of a springtime stroll through Central Park, seeing the iconic lights of Times Square, or planning an important business trip across the ocean? We’re making your travels to the USA even more convenient. We are pleased to announce that starting this April, Uzbekistan Airways is increasing the frequency of its scheduled flights on the Tashkent – New York – Tashkent route. More flights mean more freedom and flexibility in planning your perfect schedule! Tickets are already on sale! Comfortable journeys begin with Uzbekistan Airways!

title: Uzbekistan Airways and Integro Technologies Drive Crew Planning Optimization with PANORAMA COS
body: Uzbekistan Airways is the flag carrier of Uzbekistan and one of the leading airlines in Central Asia, serving over 100 destinations across Europe, Asia, and North America. In 2025, the airline carried a record 6.6 million passengers, and in November of that year placed the largest order in its history – for 22 new Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner widebody aircraft, while continuing to take delivery of Airbus A321neo jets. As part of its strategy for sustainable growth and enhanced service quality, Uzbekistan Airways plans to at least double its fleet by 2030. To support its rapid growth and boost operational efficiency, Uzbekistan Airways is banking on innovation and professionalism. Alongside the continuous training of top-tier specialists at its own flight school, the active expansion of the fleet and route network calls for the most advanced digital solutions. That is why the airline is deploying more sophisticated and highly efficient crew planning software. PANORAMA COS is a cutting-edge system that enables masterful resource management amid a growing number of flights, while ensuring full compliance with aviation regulations, stringent IOSA international safety standards, and a balanced distribution of workload among crew members. Given these factors and the need to enhance its digital capabilities, Uzbekistan Airways is deploying new tools for both day-to-day crew management and long-term planning. By building its processes around PANORAMA COS, the airline aims to achieve its overarching goal: maximizing economic efficiency during a period of explosive growth, and beyond. PANORAMA COS leverages prescriptive artificial intelligence that continuously calculates the optimal crew assignment plan, taking into account hundreds of constraints simultaneously. The implementation will enable Uzbekistan Airways to: The first phase of the implementation will optimize the pairing process with cost management capabilities, as well as crew routing, including various types of flights. The second phase will cover rostering optimization, incorporating temporary and permanent personnel attributes, historical data, individual preferences, and numerous other parameters. comments Shakhzod Ikramov, Deputy Chairman of the Board for Flight Operations at Uzbekistan Airways. says Anastasia Kornienko, Business Development Director at Integro Technologies.

title: Uzbekistan Airways marked the 690th anniversary of Amir Temur
body: Uzbekistan Airways hosted an event marking the 690th anniversary of the birth of Amir Temur. Participants discussed the rich legacy of the great commander and the Timurid era, their contribution to the development of world civilisation, and the significance of these ideas in the modern world. Particular attention was paid to issues of preserving cultural heritage, the role of leadership, and the development of science and culture. Young employees of the company took an active part – for them, this was an opportunity to learn more about their country’s history, draw inspiration from its examples and take a fresh look at national values. The figure of Amir Temur was held up as an example of a strong leader and far-sighted strategist, relevant even today. The event took place in an open and lively atmosphere.

title: The second day of “Otdykh” in Minsk
body: On the second day of the “Otdykh” International Tourism Services Exhibition and Fair, held in Minsk, a series of presentations was held focusing on Uzbekistan’s tourism potential. The presentation programme was opened by the Embassy of the Republic of Uzbekistan in Belarus, which provided participants with information about the country, its cultural heritage and tourism opportunities. During its presentation, Uzbekistan Airways outlined the company’s activities, route network and current destinations. Particular attention was paid to development prospects in the Belarusian market, including plans to increase the frequency of scheduled flights to and from Minsk. Following the presentations, a series of B2B business meetings with potential partners took place at the airline’s stand. During the talks, issues relating to the expansion of cooperation, the development of tourism links and the strengthening of interaction between Uzbekistan and Belarus were discussed. Flights on the Tashkent – Minsk – Tashkent route operate three times a week: on Mondays, Thursdays and Saturdays.

title: April 12 – The Aviation and Cosmonautics Day!
body: This memorable date is dedicated to the first human spaceflight. Today, aviation continues to connect countries and continents: thousands of flights each day link cities, shorten distances, and open new horizons for travel and communication. Congratulations on this inspiring occasion! May every flight be comfortable and every journey bright.

title: Uzbekistan Airways at the International Exhibition “Tourism and Travel”
body: Uzbekistan Airways is participating in the Kazakhstan International Tourism Fair – an important professional platform for the development of the tourism industry in Central Asia. From April 22 to 24 in Almaty, representatives of the aviation and tourism sectors are exchanging experiences, discussing current trends, and exploring new avenues for cooperation. As part of the exhibition, the airline is showcasing its capabilities, expanding route network, and modern approaches to service, while also strengthening partnerships with international and regional market players.
